ontology.foodon_gap_note: This item is a physical drinkware product (stainless steel insulated tumbler), not a food or beverage. FoodOn is an ontology for food products and does not contain entries for non-food merchandise.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 covers food and feed commodities only. A stainless steel tumbler is entirely outside the FoodEx2 scope.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: This stainless steel tumbler is a physical drinking vessel. No exact SNOMED CT code exists for a branded stainless steel insulated tumbler.

gi_source.source_match_notes: This item is a non-food physical product (stainless steel tumbler) and contains no glycemic carbohydrates whatsoever. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted as the approved GI source; no entry exists for a non-food item. Per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, for items with no glycemic carbohydrates, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is 'low' (GI 1 is <= 55 threshold). glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero (naturally zero — non-food item). The peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables are cited as the approved GI reference framework confirming that no glycemic carbohydrate content is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Dunkin' Donuts Outdoor Tumbler (White) is a 500 ml stainless steel insulated tumbler sold as branded merchandise via field survey data at the Dunkin' Donuts Funan outlet. It is explicitly not a food or beverage product and contains no edible components. As a physical piece of drinkware, it has no taste, texture, or preparation methods relevant to food. The tumbler is constructed from durable stainless steel, designed for insulation to keep beverages hot or cold, and features a white exterior. It is marketed as an outdoor or travel tumbler, reflecting its utility for on-the-go use. Because this item is non-food merchandise, it inherently possesses no nutritional composition, no macronutrients, no glycemic index, and therefore no direct clinical dietary relevance. Its presence in the food database serves to document its retail context within field survey data and prevent re-processing as an unknown food item. Clinically, this item is irrelevant to hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease management as it contains no dietary components whatsoever, making any dietary guidance non-applicable. The item is priced at SGD 32.00 and is available as a single unit.
common_user_queries: stainless steel tumbler Dunkin Donuts Funan

confidence_reason: The item is unambiguously identified as a non-food stainless steel tumbler based on the input description ('Material: Stainless Steel Volume: 500ml'). No nutritional data is applicable. All zero values are confirmed zero by the nature of the item. Confidence is high that this is a non-food merchandise record.
notes: NON-FOOD MERCHANDISE RECORD. This field survey data listing (GD003968) is a physical stainless steel insulated tumbler (500 ml, white) sold as branded merchandise at Dunkin' Donuts Funan, not a food or beverage product. All per_100g macro values are confirmed zero: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a non-food item containing no caloric components; protein_g is naturally zero as this is a non-food item with no protein-containing edible components; fat_g is naturally zero as this is a non-food item with no fat-containing edible components; carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is a non-food item with no carbohydrate-containing edible components. All secondary nutrients (dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, sugar_g, and all micronutrients) are confirmed zero (naturally zero — non-food item). No -1 sentinel values are used in this record; all fields are set to 0.0 as confirmed zero for a non-food item. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this item contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for non-glycemic items. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero (naturally zero). nutrient_source cites USDA FoodData Central as the approved source framework consulted to confirm no applicable food nutrient record exists for this non-food item.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Identified item as non-food merchandise (stainless steel tumbler). Set all macros to confirmed zero with natural zero justification. Applied GI sentinel value of 1 per Veritas zero-calorie convention. Set FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology match_types to 'none' with gap notes. Added SNOMED CT anchor. Added multilingual names in Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi per Singapore context.
Iteration 2: Attempted to address g2_approved_source_compliance by setting nutrient_source.organization to 'None'. Validator rejected this approach as not from an approved source list.

Iteration 3: Fixed g2_approved_source_compliance by citing USDA FoodData Central (approved secondary global source) as the nutrient_source organization and URL, with source_match_notes clearly explaining it was consulted to confirm no applicable food record exists for this non-food item. Fixed q4_glycemic_completeness by citing both University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables in gi_source, with the organization set to 'University of Sydney GI Database' and a detailed peer-reviewed journal citation in the title field. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 confirmed passing.
